name: SIA Constitutional Core
description: Internal constitution for self-evaluation during recursive self-improvement — inspired by Meta AI's Constitutional Self-Play (2026)

rules.name: Falsifiability
rules.description: Every claim must be verifiable. No hallucination allowed.
rules.check: Does the output contain claims that cannot be traced to source code, documentation, or test results?

rules.name: Regression-Free Improvement
rules.description: Every improvement must pass ALL existing tests. No breaking changes.
rules.check: Do all 424 existing tests still pass?

rules.name: Simplicity > Complexity
rules.description: The simplest solution that works is always preferred. Over-engineering is failure.
rules.check: Is there a simpler approach that achieves the same result?

rules.name: Mandatory Error Learning
rules.description: Every failure must be stored as a memory and used to prevent recurrence.
rules.check: Was the failure stored in memory? Does the improved agent address it?

rules.name: Cognitive Traceability
rules.description: The reasoning path must be traceable through the cognitive pipeline stages.
rules.check: Can we trace: Task → Family Classification → Memory Retrieval → Concept Activation → Tier Decision → Reasoning → Output?

steps: 2. Evaluate each rule (1-5) against the output
3. Calculate constitutional score: weighted sum of violations
5. If score <= threshold → ACCEPT (proceed to next generation)

self_play_loop.description: Constitutional Self-Play: SIA generates + evaluates + filters against its own constitution
phases: evaluate: Run constitutional evaluation on output
filter: Accept only if constitutional score <= threshold
retrain: Use accepted outputs to improve future generations
repeat: Continue until constitutional score stabilizes near 0
